mission.title: Install and Configure Kpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: kpt is a package-centric toolchain that automates Kubernetes configuration editing, simplifying the management of Kubernetes platforms and KRM-driven infrastructure. Installing kpt allows you to leverage a powerful CLI for managing configuration as data, enhancing your Kubernetes experience.

steps.title: Add the kpt Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official kpt Helm repository to your local Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add kpt https://charts.kpt.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install kpt using Helm
steps.description:
Install kpt in a new namespace called 'kpt-system':
```bash
helm install kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt-system --create-namespace --version v1.0.0-beta.61
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the kpt pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kpt-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the kpt deployment:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment kpt --namespace kpt-system --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access the kpt CLI
steps.description:
You can now access the kpt CLI by port-forwarding the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kpt --namespace kpt-system 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the kpt pod running in the 'kpt-system' namespace, and you will be able to access the kpt CLI through port-forwarding. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly on the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kpt https://charts.kpt.dev
helm repo update
helm install kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt-system --create-namespace --version v1.0.0-beta.61
kubectl set resources deployment kpt --namespace kpt-system --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
